title: The softener 一下 after verbs
pattern: Verb + 一下
en_explanation: Adding 一下 after a verb softens the tone, suggesting a brief or casual action, similar to saying 'just a bit' or 'for a moment'. It makes requests sound more polite and less demanding.
example_pinyin: Qǐng děng yīxià.
example_en: Please wait a moment.
ru_note: 别 добавляет мягкость просьбе — в русском это ближе к 'подожди-ка', но в китайском это грамматическая конструкция.

title: Progressive action with 在/正在
pattern: Subject + 在/正在 + Verb + Object + (呢)
en_explanation: To show an action is happening right now, place 在 or 正在 before the verb; the particle 呢 can be added at the end for emphasis. This is the standard way to express the progressive aspect in Chinese.
example_pinyin: Tā zhèngzài chīfàn ne.
example_en: He is eating right now.
ru_note: Похоже на русский несовершенный вид, но здесь это отдельный маркер перед глаголом, а не форма самого глагола.

title: Double 了 for change up to now
pattern: Subject + Verb/Adjective + 了 ... + Time Duration + 了
en_explanation: When 了 appears both after the verb and at the end of the sentence, it signals that a situation started in the past and is still continuing, often with a sense of change. It highlights that the state persists up to the present moment.
example_pinyin: Wǒ xué Zhōngwén liǎng nián le.
example_en: I have been studying Chinese for two years (and counting).
ru_note: Похоже на русский 'уже два года как учу' — подчёркивает, что действие продолжается до сих пор.

title: Imminent future with 要...了
pattern: Subject + 要 + Verb/Situation + 了
en_explanation: The pattern 要...了 expresses that something is about to happen very soon. It signals an imminent change or event, similar to saying 'is about to' in English.
example_pinyin: Huǒchē yào kāi le.
example_en: The train is about to leave.
ru_note: Соответствует русскому 'вот-вот' — подчёркивает, что событие произойдёт очень скоро.

title: Distinguishing 的, 得, 地
pattern: Noun-modifier + 的 + Noun | Verb/Adj + 得 + Complement | Adverb-modifier + 地 + Verb
en_explanation: All three particles are pronounced 'de' but serve different grammatical roles: 的 links a modifier to a noun, 得 introduces a complement after a verb or adjective, and 地 links an adverbial modifier to a verb. Choosing the right one depends on what follows.
example_zh: 他跑得很快，高兴地笑了。
example_pinyin: Tā pǎo de hěn kuài, gāoxìng de xiào le.
example_en: He runs very fast, and laughed happily.
ru_note: Все три звучат одинаково 'de', но пишутся разными иероглифами в зависимости от функции — сложность именно в письме.

title: Prohibition with 别
pattern: 别 + Verb + (Object)
en_explanation: 别 before a verb means 'don't', forming a direct prohibition or negative command. It is often softened by adding 了 at the end of the sentence.
example_pinyin: Bié dānxīn le.
example_en: Don't worry.
ru_note: Прямой эквивалент русского повелительного отрицания 'не делай' — ставится перед глаголом.

title: Cause and effect with 因为...所以
pattern: 因为 + Reason, 所以 + Result
en_explanation: This paired conjunction connects a cause with its result: 因为 introduces the reason, and 所以 introduces the consequence. Either half can sometimes be omitted, but using both makes the logic clearest.
example_pinyin: Yīnwèi xiàyǔ, suǒyǐ wǒ méi qù.
example_en: Because it rained, I didn't go.
ru_note: Как русское 'потому что...поэтому', но в китайском принято использовать оба союза вместе.

title: Concession with 虽然...但是
pattern: 虽然 + Clause A, 但是 + Clause B
en_explanation: This paired conjunction expresses contrast: 虽然 introduces a fact, and 但是 introduces a surprising or contrasting result despite that fact. It corresponds to 'although...but' in English.
example_zh: 虽然很累，但是我很开心。
example_pinyin: Suīrán hěn lèi, dànshì wǒ hěn kāixīn.
example_en: Although I'm tired, I'm very happy.
ru_note: Похоже на русское 'хотя...но', и оба союза обычно сохраняются вместе, в отличие от английского.

title: Range with 从...到
pattern: 从 + Start Point + 到 + End Point
en_explanation: 从...到 marks the beginning and end of a range, whether in time or space, meaning 'from...to'. It works the same way for locations, dates, or times.
example_zh: 我从九点到十二点上课。
example_pinyin: Wǒ cóng jiǔ diǎn dào shí'èr diǎn shàngkè.
example_en: I have class from nine to twelve o'clock.
ru_note: Работает как русское 'от...до' и для времени, и для места, что упрощает изучение.

title: Distance with 离
pattern: Place A + 离 + Place B + Distance/Adjective
en_explanation: The preposition 离 expresses the distance between two places, meaning 'from' in the sense of spatial separation. It is followed by an adjective like 远 (far) or 近 (near), or a specific distance.
example_pinyin: Xuéxiào lí wǒ jiā hěn jìn.
example_en: The school is very close to my home.
ru_note: 离 обозначает удалённость, похоже на русское 'от' в 'близко от дома', но используется только для расстояния, не для движения.

title: Distributive 每 with measure words
pattern: 每 + Measure Word + Noun + 都 + Verb
en_explanation: 每 means 'every' and must be paired with a measure word before the noun; the adverb 都 usually follows to mean 'all' or 'each one without exception'. This combination emphasizes universality.
example_pinyin: Měi ge xuésheng dōu hěn nǔlì.
example_en: Every student works hard.
ru_note: 每 требует счётного слова, как и обычные числительные, а 都 обязательно подтверждает 'все без исключения'.

title: Choice questions with 还是
pattern: Option A + 还是 + Option B?
en_explanation: 还是 is used inside a question to mean 'or', offering a choice between two alternatives that the listener must pick from. It differs from 或者, which is used for 'or' in statements, not questions.
example_pinyin: Nǐ yào chá háishi kāfēi?
example_en: Do you want tea or coffee?
ru_note: 还是 используется только в вопросах выбора, аналог русского 'или' в вопросе 'чай или кофе?'.

title: Statement 'or' with 或者
pattern: Option A + 或者 + Option B
en_explanation: 或者 also means 'or', but it is used in statements rather than questions, presenting alternatives without asking the listener to choose. It cannot replace 还是 in a question.
example_pinyin: Nǐ kěyǐ hē chá huòzhě kāfēi.
example_en: You can drink tea or coffee.
ru_note: 或者 подходит только для утверждений, а не вопросов — русское 'или' не делает такого различия.

title: Sequence with 先...再
pattern: 先 + Action A, 再 + Action B
en_explanation: 先...再 expresses the order of two actions, meaning 'first...then'. It clarifies that Action A must happen before Action B in a planned sequence.
example_pinyin: Wǒ xiān chīfàn, zài xuéxí.
example_en: I'll eat first, then study.
ru_note: Прямой аналог русского 'сначала...потом' для описания последовательности действий.

title: 有点儿 vs 一点儿
pattern: 有点儿 + Adjective (negative) | Adjective + 一点儿 (comparison)
en_explanation: 有点儿 comes before an adjective and usually expresses a slightly negative feeling, meaning 'a bit too'. 一点儿 comes after an adjective, often in comparisons, meaning 'a little' or 'slightly'.
example_zh: 这件衣服有点儿贵，那件便宜一点儿。
example_pinyin: Zhè jiàn yīfu yǒudiǎnr guì, nà jiàn piányi yīdiǎnr.
example_en: This piece of clothing is a bit too expensive, that one is a little cheaper.
ru_note: 有点儿 обычно про недовольство ('слишком'), а 一点儿 — про небольшую разницу, часто в сравнении.

title: Verb reduplication V一V
pattern: Verb + 一 + Verb
en_explanation: Repeating a verb with 一 in between, such as 看一看, softens the action and suggests trying it briefly or casually, similar in feeling to 一下. It is common with single-syllable action verbs.
example_pinyin: Nǐ cháng yī cháng zhège cài.
example_en: Try tasting this dish.
ru_note: Повтор глагола смягчает просьбу, похоже на русское 'попробуй-ка' — придаёт лёгкость и непринуждённость.

title: Emphasis with 是...的
pattern: Subject + 是 + Detail + Verb + 的
en_explanation: The 是...的 construction emphasizes specific details of a past action already known to have happened, such as the time, place, or manner. It does not question whether the action occurred, only highlights how, when, or where.
example_pinyin: Wǒ shì zuótiān lái de.
example_en: It was yesterday that I came.
ru_note: Выделяет обстоятельство уже известного действия, похоже на русское 'именно вчера я и приехал'.

title: Topic-comment sentence order
pattern: Topic + Comment (Subject + Verb + Object)
en_explanation: Chinese frequently places the topic of a sentence first, even if it is not the grammatical subject, and then comments on it afterward. This lets speakers highlight what the sentence is about before describing it.
example_pinyin: Zhè běn shū wǒ kànguo le.
example_en: This book, I have already read (it).
ru_note: Похоже на русский вынос темы в начало 'эту книгу я уже читал' — тема ставится перед комментарием.

title: Placement of 都 and 也
pattern: Subject + 都/也 + Verb Phrase
en_explanation: Adverbs like 都 (all) and 也 (also) must be placed directly before the verb, never before the subject. When both appear together, 也 usually comes before 都 in the sentence.
example_pinyin: Wǒmen yě dōu xǐhuan Zhōngwén.
example_en: We all also like Chinese.
ru_note: 都 и 也 всегда стоят перед глаголом, а не перед подлежащим — это отличается от гибкого русского порядка.

title: 怎么 vs 怎么样
pattern: 怎么 + Verb? | Subject/Situation + 怎么样?
en_explanation: 怎么 asks 'how' before a verb, inquiring about the method or reason for doing something. 怎么样 asks for an opinion or evaluation about something, similar to 'how is it?' or 'how about?'.
example_zh: 这个字怎么写？这部电影怎么样？
example_pinyin: Zhège zì zěnme xiě? Zhè bù diànyǐng zěnmeyàng?
example_en: How do you write this character? How is this movie?
ru_note: 怎么 спрашивает о способе действия, а 怎么样 — о мнении или оценке, как русское 'как это?' в смысле 'как тебе?'.

title: Degree questions with 多 + adjective
pattern: 多 + Adjective?
en_explanation: 多 before an adjective forms a question about degree or measurement, such as age, height, or distance, meaning 'how...'. It is commonly used with adjectives like 大, 高, and 远.
example_pinyin: Nǐ jīnnián duō dà?
example_en: How old are you this year?
ru_note: 多 + прилагательное соответствует русским вопросам вроде 'насколько большой' или 'сколько тебе лет'.

title: 会 vs 能 vs 可以 in context
pattern: Subject + 会/能/可以 + Verb + Object
en_explanation: 会 refers to a learned skill, 能 refers to physical or circumstantial ability, and 可以 refers to permission or what is allowed. Choosing correctly depends on whether you mean skill, capability, or permission.
example_zh: 我能来，但是今天不可以迟到。
example_pinyin: Wǒ néng lái, dànshì jīntiān bù kěyǐ chídào.
example_en: I can come, but today I'm not allowed to be late.
ru_note: Три оттенка 'мочь' — навык, возможность и разрешение — в русском часто передаются одним словом 'могу'.

title: 给 as a preposition meaning 'to/for'
pattern: Subject + 给 + Person + Verb + Object
en_explanation: 给 before a person indicates who benefits from or receives the action, meaning 'to' or 'for' that person. It is placed before the verb, unlike English where 'to' often comes after.
example_pinyin: Wǒ gěi māma dǎ diànhuà.
example_en: I call my mom (call to my mom).
ru_note: 给 ставится перед глаголом и означает 'для/кому', похоже на русский дательный падеж 'маме'.

title: Contrast of speed with 就 and 才
pattern: Subject + 就/才 + Verb + Object
en_explanation: 就 suggests that something happens quickly, early, or easily, while 才 suggests that something happens slowly, late, or only under certain conditions. The choice reflects the speaker's attitude toward the timing.
example_zh: 他六点就到了，我八点才到。
example_pinyin: Tā liù diǎn jiù dào le, wǒ bā diǎn cái dào.
example_en: He arrived at six already, but I didn't arrive until eight.
ru_note: 就 передаёт 'уже так рано/быстро', а 才 — 'только теперь, поздно' — тонкое различие в оценке времени.

title: Resultative complements 完 and 好
pattern: Verb + 完/好 + (Object)
en_explanation: Adding 完 or 好 right after a verb shows that the action was completed successfully. 完 emphasizes finishing an activity entirely, while 好 emphasizes that the result is now ready or properly done.
example_zh: 我写完了作业，准备好了。
example_pinyin: Wǒ xiěwán le zuòyè, zhǔnbèi hǎo le.
example_en: I finished writing my homework, I'm ready.
ru_note: 完 и 好 после глагола показывают завершённость результата, похоже на русские приставки 'до-' и 'с-' (дописал, сготовил).
